description: Air Products and Chemicals is a global industrial gases supplier focused on atmospheric, process, and specialty gases, related equipment, and long-term supply services for heavy industry.

overview.title: Business Profile
paragraphs: Air Products and Chemicals supplies industrial gases and related equipment to customers in refining, chemicals, metals, electronics, manufacturing, food, healthcare, and energy markets. Its portfolio includes atmospheric gases such as nitrogen, oxygen, and argon, as well as process gases including hydrogen, helium, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and syngas.

The company’s business is not glamorous in the consumer-internet sense, which is probably a point in its favor: it sells molecules, infrastructure, and reliability to customers that cannot simply reboot a plant when supply fails.

overview.title: Hydrogen Position
paragraphs: Hydrogen is a central part of Air Products’ identity and growth narrative. The company has long supplied industrial hydrogen to refineries and chemical customers, while also pursuing large clean hydrogen and ammonia projects aimed at hard-to-abate industrial and transportation demand.

For Free The World, hydrogen should be treated as both a core industrial input and a contested energy-transition platform: potentially useful, capital-intensive, and surrounded by enough policy optimism to require sober source tracking.

overview.title: Nitrogen And Atmospheric Gases
paragraphs: Liquid nitrogen fits within Air Products’ broader atmospheric gases business, which uses air separation and distribution networks to serve customers requiring cooling, inerting, blanketing, freezing, purging, and process-control applications.

Nitrogen is a strong registry candidate because it is foundational infrastructure rather than a branded end-user product. Mapping alternatives should therefore focus on supply models, production methods, energy intensity, locality, resilience, and whether open or decentralized approaches can reduce dependence on large incumbent gas networks.

overview.title: Registry Relevance
paragraphs: Air Products sits in a sector where freedom questions are less about app-store lock-in and more about physical dependence: centralized production assets, long-term contracts, proprietary process know-how, and logistics networks.

The initial product map should begin with industrial hydrogen and liquid nitrogen, then expand cautiously into oxygen, argon, helium, carbon dioxide, syngas, specialty gases, on-site gas generation, gas equipment, and clean hydrogen projects as source quality permits.
